About the Center on U.S.-China RelationsIn seeking new ways of building mutual understanding between the United States and China, the Center undertakes projects and events which explore areas of common interest and divergent views between the two countries, focusing on policy, culture, business, media, economics, energy and the environment.

Connect With Us

Asia Society Center on U.S.-China Relations X Account Asia Society Center on U.S.-China Relations Facebook Account

Highlights

Events

Upcoming and past events from the Asia Society Center on U.S.-China Relations

report

Allies and Partners: The Future of Relations with China

A new Task Force on U.S.-China Policy Working Group report published with the German Council on Foreign Relations on converging and diverging approaches to China among traditional allied countries.

Baifang Schell Book Prize

The Baifang Schell Book Prize is an annual awards, started in 2024, that celebrates exceptional book-length works on or from China and the Sinophone world, published in English.

paper

Rare Earths and Strategic Competition

Sam George, Senior Fellow at the Center on U.S.-China Relations, explains how China built this leverage, how the U.S. system failed to mobilize, and assesses the Trump administration's latest moves to de-risk its minerals supply chain.

report

Memo on U.S. Policy to China

A memo on optimizing U.S. policy toward China by Task Force on U.S.-China Policy co-chair Susan Shirk and Task Force member Rick Waters.

New York

COAL + ICE Opens at Asia Society in New York

COAL + ICE opened on February 13 at Asia Society New York. Exhibition photographers and curators speak on what they want visitors to walk away with, what inspired their work, and what makes COAL + ICE special.

report

Prioritizing Southeast Asia in American China Policy

A new Task Force on U.S.-China Policy report, Prioritizing Southeast Asia in American China Policy, lays out the challenges and recommends actions for the U.S. government.

report

Silicon Triangle

A new report by Asia Society and the Hoover Institution offers recommendations for how the United States and its allies can ensure a reliable supply of semiconductors, when most of the world’s semiconductor supply comes from Taiwan, and Tai

Coexistence 2.0: U.S.-China Relations in a Changing World

Experts from the Task Force on U.S.-China Policy convene at Harvard University for a symposium on the future of U.S.-China Relations

Avoiding War Over Taiwan

A policy brief by members of the Task Force on US-China Policy arguing that despite rising tensions between China and Taiwan and the United States, it is both essential and possible to avoid war in the Taiwan Strait.

Statement: Hardheaded Diplomacy with China on Ukraine

A statement by members of the Task Force on U.S.-China Policy on diplomacy with China in the ongoing Ukraine crisis

Videos

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

2026 China Books Review Book Prize Winners

Barbara Demick, Fang Fang, and Michael Berry are awarded the nonfiction and literary awards, respectively, for this year’s Baifang Schell Book Prize.

Watch Now

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

The Two-Party Problem: A Conversation with KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun

The KMT Chairwoman discusses the future of cross-strait relations, the prospects for dialogue with Beijing, and whether a stable status quo can be maintained amid rising tensions and growing uncertainty across the Taiwan Strait.

Watch Now

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

Frank Dikötter: How Communism Won China

Frank Dikötter joins Orville Schell to discuss the rise of the Chinese Communist Party from 1921 to 1949 and the arguments behind his book "Red Dawn Over China".

Watch Now

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

From Korean Messiah to China’s 21st Century Helmsman

Jonathan Cheng discusses his new book which explores the surprising links between Kim Il Sung and American Christianity, and what North Korea’s personality cult reveals about global political dynamics today.

Watch Now

Arts

 / 
New York

[In Chinese] 725 Salon: Lung Ying-tai

Join Lung Ying-tai and Ouyang Bin for an intimate 725 Salon conversation, tracing her journey from a fearless public voice to a reflective storyteller shaped by history, family, and the quiet rhythms of life.

Watch Now

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

Trump-Xi Summit 2026: What to Expect for U.S.–China Relations

China experts examine the strategic priorities, diplomatic dynamics, and broader implications surrounding the U.S.–China leaders’ meeting.

Watch Now

Current Affairs

 / 
New York

Yi-Ling Liu: China’s Internet Censorship

The technology journalist examines how China’s internet became a contested space of creativity, connection, and control behind the Great Firewall.

Watch Now

Upcoming Events

In-person

Panel Discussion

Hong Kong’s Past, Present and Future

30 Jun 2026
6:30 - 8 p.m.

This panel brings together authors and observers of Hong Kong to reflect on the city's past, consider its present, and speculate about its future.

Past Events

In-person

Discussion

[In Chinese] 725 Salon: David Weng

15 Jun 2026
6:30 - 8 p.m.

Join David Weng, host of the podcast Qi zhu lou yan bin ke and a veteran finance professional, for a conversation on long-term economic cycles and how individuals can navigate periods of uncertainty. [In Mandarin]

In-person

Discussion

The Two Party Problem: A Conversation with KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun

08 Jun 2026
6:30 - 8 p.m.

Join us for a candid conversation with KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun, as she reflects on the possible future of cross-strait relations. She will be joined in the discussion by Daniel Russel and Orville Schell.

In-person

Discussion

Frank Dikötter: How Communism Won China

02 Jun 2026
6:30 - 8 p.m.

Register now to join us for the book launch of Red Dawn Over China and understand the origins of China’s communist revolution. Frank Dikötter will be in discussion with Orville Schell.

In-person

Discussion

Book Club: Tahir Hamut Izgil

27 May 2026
5:30 - 7 p.m.

Join the China Books Review book club in May to discuss Uyghur poet-in-exile Tahir Hamut Izgil's memoir Waiting To be Arrested at Night, a harrowing account of one family's escape from the persecution of the Uyghur people in Xinjiang.

About the Center on U.S.-China RelationsIn seeking new ways of building mutual understanding between the United States and China, the Center undertakes projects and events which explore areas of common interest and divergent views between the two countries, focusing on policy, culture, business, media, economics, energy and the environment.
